channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, AAMER MADHANI, and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: NYC mayor deflects questions about bribery charges as a potential witness speaks outside City Hall
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his most thorough public defense to date since his indictment on federal bribery charges, insisting the case was a “test” that would not distract him from governing as he deflected specific questions about the investigation for more than an ho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets,’ with a new color
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween, and a new color has joined the lineup this year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Daniel Day-Lewis ends acting retirement for a movie directed by his son
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Daniel Day-Lewis is coming out of retirement, seven years after his last movie, for a film directed by his son Ronan Day-Lewis. The project was announced Tuesday by Focus Features and Plan B, who are partnering on “Anemone.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Students say they're fed up with Ohio high school bathroom policy
item.description: "Today, I had to use the bathroom so bad and my teacher gave me permission to go run there real quick and all of them were locked," Freshman Nora Hardesty said.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, NewsNation

item.title: Workday down? User reports indicate problems with platform
item.description: Is your Workday down? You might not be alone. According to Downdetector.com, reports of people having problems with Workday spiked shortly after 10 a.m. Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, Fox 5

item.title: Helene's death toll surpasses 150 as crews continue to search for survivors
item.description: Exhausted emergency workers continued to work around-the-clock to clear roads, restore power and cellphone service, and reach people left stranded by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, KRON4

item.title: 2 Martha’s Vineyard illegal immigrants arrested on same day in latest ICE busts on wealthy, liberal islands
item.description: A drug trafficker from Brazil and a Salvadoran accused of crimes against humanity in his own country, both in the country illegally, were taken into custody on Martha's Vineyard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Trump woo Gen Z voters in critical swing states
item.description: The Harris and Trump campaigns are stepping up their outreach to Generation Z voters in critical battlegrounds, targeting a voting bloc expected to play a key role in a tight White House race where any marginal advantage gained could make the difference.
item.dc:creator: Yash Roy, NewsNation

item.title: Eric Adams calls out alleged collusion of feds and mainstream media, asks judge for 'consequences'
item.description: Lawyers for New York City Mayor Eric Adams are seeking sanctions against the Department of Justice, which they accuse of leaking details of his indictment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baseball legend Pete Rose dies at 83
item.description: Pete Rose, Major League Baseball’s all-time hits leader who endured a lifetime ban for betting on the sport, has died at age 83, his agent Jeff Lenkov confirmed to KTLA 5 News. According to TMZ, Rose died at his home in Las Vegas on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Salt Life is closing all stores across 10 states: See the full list of locations
item.description: Salt Life, a brand synonymous with Florida’s ocean-loving culture, is closing all of its physical stores as part of a significant transition to a wholesale and e-commerce business model.
item.dc:creator: Brody Wooddell, WGN 9

item.title: FBI to pay $22M to settle claims of sexual discrimination at training academy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI agreed Monday to pay more than $22 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging female recruits were singled out for dismissal in training and routinely harassed by instructors with sexually charged comments about their breast size, false allegations of infidelity and
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Submerged EVs can catch fire when inundated with saltwater: What to know
item.description: Electric vehicles can catch fire if they are inundated by saltwater, so owners who live in the path of a major storm should take precautions and prepare for the possibility that they'll be unable to charge their cars during a power outage.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, KRON4

item.title: These are the 50 best school districts in America for 2025, report finds
item.description: Choosing where to live could come down to the strength of the school district for many parents, and a new study ranks the top 50 in the nation.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Man is sentenced to 35 years for shooting 2 Jewish men as they left Los Angeles synagogues
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California man was sentenced Monday to 35 years in prison for shooting and wounding two Jewish men as they left synagogues in Los Angeles last year, federal prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: When do hurricane names get retired?
item.description: With nearly two months to go until the end of what was predicted to be a "hyperactive" hurricane season, many residents along Florida's Gulf Coast and southeastern states are grappling with the damage and destruction left in the wake of the latest hurricane.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, Fox 5

item.title: Oasis announce 3 US tour dates: Where and when are they playing?
item.description: Fans were expecting the band to schedule stops in North America after billboards teasing their reunion began popping up in U.S. and Canadian cities.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Donate today to help Red Cross efforts across 10 states
item.description: Days after Hurricane Helene's deadly force tore a path north from the Gulf Coast, many Americans remained without power, water and other basic necessities.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Republican 'absolutely certain' there will be supplemental to address Helene aid
item.description: Rep. Kat Cammack (R-Fla.) said Monday she’s “absolutely certain” Congress will consider a supplemental aid package to provide additional disaster relief to areas devastated by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suspect pleads not guilty in attempted assassination
item.description: The man accused of attempting to assassinate former President Trump at one of his Florida golf courses earlier this month has pleaded not guilty to five federal charges, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: California governor vetoes bill to create first-in-nation AI safety measures
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom vetoed a landmark bill aimed at establishing first-in-the-nation safety measures for large artificial intelligence models Sunday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kris Kristofferson, country music superstar and actor, dies at 88
item.description: Kris Kristofferson, a Rhodes scholar with a deft writing style and rough charisma who became a country music superstar and A-list Hollywood actor, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tropical Weather Latest: US Southeast struggles to recover in Hurricane Helene's aftermath
item.description: Massive rains brought by Hurricane Helene have left many people stranded or homeless as the cleanup begins from the monster tempest that killed at least 84 people. Helene has caused billions of dollars in destruction across a wide swath of the U.S. Southeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supplies, emergency workers rushed to North Carolina while Florida digs out from Helene's damage
item.description: The U.S. Southeast grappled Sunday with rising death tolls, lack of vital supplies in isolated, flood-stricken areas and widespread loss of homes and property while the devastating toll of Hurricane Helene became clear to officials who warned of a lengthy and difficult rebuild.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Exploring the Eric Adams indictment for bribery, wire fraud, and solicitating contributions from Turkish nationals
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams pleaded not guilty in a federal bribery case after being indicted on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US retailers brace for potential pain from a longshoremen's strike
item.description: With a dockworkers' strike threatening to close ports on the East and Gulf coasts beginning this week, Chris Butler is growing worried.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Price of Mega Millions tickets to go up: Here's how much
item.description: PIERRE, S.D. (KELO) -- Some big changes are coming soon for the Mega Millions lotto game, whether or not the South Dakota Lottery Commission or any other state that sells the tickets agrees with them.
item.dc:creator: Bob Mercer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dunkin' is giving out free coffee for National Coffee Day
item.description: Happy National Coffee day to all of those who need a caffeine kick in the mornings. If you're still searching for your fix on Sunday morning, look no further than Dunkin'. The popular coffee and donut chain is giving out free coffee to its reward members in celebration of National Coffee Day.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Profiles in clean energy: Former Shell VP helps create a new way of making clean electricity
item.description: When Cindy Taff was a vice president at the giant oil and gas company Shell in Houston, her middle schooler Brianna would sometimes look over her shoulder as she worked from home. “Why are you still working in oil and gas?” her daughter asked more than once.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3,600 chemicals from food packaging found in people: Research
item.description: PFAs resist breaking down and can build up in the body over time, earning them the nickname "forever chemicals.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KTLA 5

item.title: Taylor Swift approval among Republicans crashes after Harris endorsement
item.description: While 28 percent of Republicans last year had responded that they had a positive view of Swift, Friday's poll found just 12 percent expressed the same warm feeling towards the 34-year-old Grammy Award winner, NBC reported.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, ABC 4

item.title: Campaign says Robinson in 'good spirits' after being hospitalized, treated for burns
item.description: The lieutenant governor burned his hand after leaning against a truck at a truck show in Mt Airy, according to a Washington Post reporter.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Footage of motorcade racing JFK to the hospital after he was shot sells for $137,500 at auction
item.description: Newly emerged film footage of President John F. Kennedy's motorcade speeding down a Dallas freeway toward a hospital after he was fatally wounded sold at auction Saturday for $137,500.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, Fox 5
